pattern: Instead of summing every odd-length window directly, count how many odd-length windows each index participates in and weight that index's value by that count.
hints: Summing every odd-length window directly works, but there's a way to avoid re-summing overlapping windows by thinking per-index instead of per-subarray.
For index i, any choice of a start in [0,i] and an end in [i,n-1] defines a subarray containing i; only the ones with odd (end-start+1) length matter for the answer.
The number of subarrays containing i is (i+1)*(n-i); roughly half of those have odd length, precisely ceil of that product over 2 — multiply that count by arr[i] and sum over all i.

edge_inputs.description: single element has exactly one odd-length subarray, itself
two elements: only the two length-1 subarrays are odd length
all-equal values still weight each index by its distinct subarray count
minimal length-3 array with distinct values
longer array approaching typical stress sizes

stress.description: random array up to the length cap
stress.note: size is clamped to the 100-length constraint
